result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would remove the human capacity for self-deception—the ability to ignore or rationalize away evidence that contradicts our comfort, identity, or power. Not to force honesty, but to make it impossible to lie to ourselves.

That one shift would ripple through everything: no more denial of climate science, no more justifying cruelty with ideology, no more choosing ignorance because the truth is inconvenient. Leaders could still lie to others, but they couldn’t sustain the lies they tell themselves. We’d still make mistakes, but we’d have to face them.

The world would become terrifyingly honest—and perhaps, for the first time, capable of real change.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
